item.title: Chemically Informed Representations Enable Prediction of Amphiphilic Copolymer Solution Behavior
item.description: The high-dimensional design landscape characteristic of synthetic polymers challenges the rational design of functional materials. While machine learning models can enable efficient data-driven navigation of these landscapes, representation strategies ...

item.title: GVIM 2.0: A Multi-Agent Research System for Chemistry and Materials Science
item.description: Large language models are increasingly used as natural-language interfaces for chemistry and materials research, but their utility in scientific workflows depends on their ability to connect user requests with executable tools, structured data handling, ...

item.title: Water clustering in hydrophobic zeolitic imidazolate frameworks under pressure
item.description: Understanding water in hydrophobic nanoscale environments is central to problems ranging from biological recognition and ion transport to geological fluid confinement and nanofluidic energy absorption. Pressurised intrusion into hydrophobic metal–organic ...

item.title: How Far Can Machine Learning Guide Catalyst Discovery?
item.description: Machine learning is increasingly used to guide catalyst discovery, but it remains unclear how much reported performance reflects genuine predictive ability. In this work, the evaluation protocol alone is shown to manufacture or erase apparent success. ...

item.title: Co-solvent enhanced solvation shell lability improves ion transport in Li-ion battery electrolytes
item.description: Electrolytes for extreme fast charging (XFC) of Li-ion batteries must combine high ionic conductivity with favorable Li+transference, yet these transport properties are difficult to improve simultaneously in practical carbonate-based liquids. Here, we ...

item.title: From Random Accuracy to Chemical Generalization: A Benchmark for Dye Optical-Property Prediction
item.description: Machine learning is accelerating the design of functional dyes, but reported errors are often dominated by random train–test splits that mix related chromophores, solvents and literature sources. This makes it unclear whether models can support discovery ...

item.title: Graph-Transformer for Accurate Transition-State Geometries and Barriers in Large Chemical Reactions
item.description: Despite rapid advances in machine learning for transition state prediction, nearly all existing methods remain restricted to small organic molecules containing only a handful of elements and fewer than 30 atoms. Extending transition state prediction to ...

item.title: Machine Learning as a Lens for Breaking Scaling Relations in Heterogeneous Catalysis: Strategies, Evidence, and Limits
item.description: Linear scaling relations and the Sabatier principle impose a fundamental ceiling on the activity of heterogeneous catalysts: because the binding energies of related intermediates are coupled, optimising one weakens another, and the best catalyst sits at a ...

item.title: Induced Structural Disorder Enables High Ionic Conductivity and Capacity in Li2Fe1−xMnxCl4 Cathodes
item.description: The development of high energy density solid-state batteries requires the discovery of ionically conductive cathode active materials which enable the production of electrode composites with high cathode active material loadings. Here, we show that an ...

item.title: Activating Interstitial Oxygen Conduction in Sr3V2O8 Palmierite Oxides through Minimal Mo Substitution
item.description: The palmierite oxides are a promising family of ion conductors, and understanding how compositional modification affects their structure and, in turn, their ionic conductivity is pivotal for unlocking high ionic conductivity in these solid electrolytes. ...

item.title: PhytoClip: Multimodal Wearable Sensing and Online Machine Learning for Real-Time Plant Health Monitoring and Early Stress Detection
item.description: Wearable plant sensing systems for simultaneous biochemical and physical monitoring with real-time multimodal data analysis remain limited. Here, we present PhytoClip, a multimodal wearable patch that continuously monitors leaf temperature, humidity, ...

item.title: ChemicHAL – agentic explainable artificial intelligence platform for deep learning in chemistry
item.description: Applications of machine learning (ML) in the natural sciences and interdisciplinary research typically benefit from downstream analyses using explainable artificial intelligence (XAI) to rationalize predictions. We have designed and implemented an AI ...

item.title: Agentic, Explainable, and Generative Artificial Intelligence for Structure-Aware Small-Molecule Drug Design
item.description: Artificial intelligence for drug discovery is moving from static virtual-screening scores toward integrated decision systems that generate molecules, predict potency, explain model behaviour, quantify uncertainty, and guide experimental prioritisation. ...

item.title: Morphology and Ion Transport in Zwitterionic Polysiloxanes
item.description: energy barriers for ion transport and therefore increase room temperature ionic conductivity. Here, 28 we demonstrate that superionic conductivity is not a universal behavior of doped PZIs, instead 29 demanding strategic design of ion transport pathways. ...

item.title: Generalized Machine Learning Potentials for Water Adsorption in Al-Based Metal–Organic Frameworks Considering Framework Flexibility
item.description: Metal–organic frameworks (MOFs) are promising materials for adsorption and separation, but accurately predicting water adsorption remains a major challenge in molecular simulation. Classical force fields, such as UFF, often fail to capture the strong, ...
